key_insight: TANS-FO closes the gap between clinical text and orthosis geometry by using a text-aligned generative model plus a real-time biomechanical surrogate to automate customized foot orthosis design.
engineering_relevance: The work demonstrates a fast, modular design-to-validation pipeline for patient-specific lattice insoles, reducing reliance on manual CAD and expensive finite-element iteration.
method: Text-Aligned Neural Surrogate maps clinical prescriptions to continuous lattice-density fields via cross-attention.
Graph Neural Network surrogate predicts plantar stress in real time as a replacement for FEA.
Closed-loop pipeline generates manufacturing-ready orthoses and checks biomechanical response within minutes.
evidence_signal: On the PicoFoot-5K dataset, the GNN surrogate matched Abaqus with R² = 0.94, and the full pipeline reported 34.7% peak-pressure reduction versus parametric CAD with 0.42 mm fit error; the small VAS study (n=12) is preliminary only.
watch_item: The prototype is not a clinically validated therapy, and the observational comfort results lack a control group and should not be interpreted as proof of efficacy.
paper_title: A Research Prototype for Closed-Loop Generative Design of Customized Foot Orthoses via Semantic-Physics Alignment
